items.title: Deta's Surf app combines the functionalities of an AI-powered browser with a knowledge management and note-taking system, akin to NotebookLM. This integration aims to provide users with an enhanced browsing experience that leverages artificial intelligence, alongside robust capabilities for organizing and synthesizing information directly within the application.

items.title: Prickly Pear Health is set to showcase its contributions to women's brain health at TechCrunch Disrupt 2025. The company will highlight how its efforts are specifically helping to improve and support women's neurological well-being.

items.title: Gusto acquired 401(k) provider Guideline for $600 million, a deal that significantly expands its retirement services offerings. Following the acquisition, Gusto reportedly plans to divest Guideline customers who are currently integrated with rival payroll platforms. This strategic move aims to consolidate Gusto's market position while streamlining its customer base.
items.summary: Gusto acquired 401(k) provider Guideline for $600 million, a deal that significantly expands its retirement services offerings. This strategic move aims to consolidate Gusto's market position while streamlining its customer base.

items.title: Waymo has received an extension to continue its robotaxi testing operations in New York City. This approval allows the autonomous vehicle company to conduct its trials until the end of 2025.

items.title: CharacterAI has removed all Disney-related characters from its platform after receiving a cease-and-desist letter from Disney. The artificial intelligence chatbot service previously allowed users to interact with AI versions of popular Disney characters. This action follows Disney's assertion of its intellectual property rights.

items.title: A Whizz co-founder claims that Donald Trump's crackdown efforts in Chicago are significantly impacting the city's delivery services. These enforcement actions are reportedly scaring delivery workers, leading them to avoid working on the streets.

items.title: Hollywood is expressing significant disapproval towards Tilly Norwood, a recently introduced AI-generated actress. This negative reception stems from concerns over job displacement for human actors, the ethical implications of using artificial intelligence in creative roles, and potential impacts on artistic integrity within the industry.

items.title: Chamath Palihapitiya has publicly warned retail investors to avoid his new Special Purpose Acquisition Company (SPAC), IPOD. He specifically cautioned against investing, citing the inherent risks and potential volatility associated with such blank-check companies, especially for individual investors.

items.title: Anker's Eufy security camera brand offered to pay users to share videos from their home surveillance devices to train its AI. The program, which compensated participants with a gift card, aimed to improve the AI's ability to detect people, vehicles, and pets. This initiative raised privacy concerns, particularly given Eufy's past claims of keeping user data strictly local and private.

items.title: Instagram's head, Adam Mosseri, firmly denies the company uses device microphones to listen to user conversations for ad targeting or content suggestions. He explains that Instagram's sophisticated AI, leveraging vast amounts of user data like activity, likes, and followers, makes such direct audio surveillance unnecessary. This AI-driven data collection provides more than enough information to predict user interests and deliver relevant content and advertisements.
items.summary: Instagram's head, Adam Mosseri, firmly denies the company uses device microphones to listen to user conversations for ad targeting or content suggestions. He explains that Instagram's sophisticated AI, leveraging vast amounts of user data like activity, likes, and followers, makes such direct audio surveillance unnecessary. This AI-driven data collection provides more than enough information to predict user interests and deliver relevant content and advertisements.

items.title: Visa crackdowns are increasingly hindering students' study-abroad aspirations, particularly for those from India. In response, India's Leverage Edu is helping these affected students by rerouting them to alternative countries and programs, enabling them to pursue international education despite visa challenges.
